Ireland Edition
Hi, I'm Pia!
"Data-driven. Culture-obsessed.
Chronically online for a reason."

A digital marketing strategist with experience managing paid media campaigns, client relationships, and cross-channel optimisation for global brands across sectors.

I build advertiser strategies grounded in audience insight and data-driven thinking, turning campaign performance into meaningful business growth.

Pia
Turn
A Q&A With Pia
The mind behind the moodboard.
So, how did you get into paid media in the first place?
Moving to the UK from India as a student meant navigating everything from scratch and honestly, the ads helped. They introduced me to things I did not know I needed. Studying strategic marketing while living that experience made the connection feel obvious. Paid media was the thing I was already paying attention to. Turning it into a career felt less like a decision and more like an inevitability.
Which platform do you enjoy working on most, and why?
I'd probably say TikTok and not just as an advertiser. As a consumer, it genuinely feels different from everything else. The way content moves, the way culture forms there in real time, the way ads can either fit perfectly or stick out completely, it keeps you honest as a strategist. That said, running campaigns across all major social platforms is where I learn the most. Each one tells you something different about your audience.
Where are you headed next, professionally?
Hopefully to Ireland, working in media, building performance creative strategies, and running campaigns that do not feel forced. The kind of work where an ad lands in someone's scroll and feels like it belongs there rather than interrupting it. That is the standard I want to hold myself to, and I think the market here is the right place to do it.
We've heard 2025 was an exciting year for you! What made it special?
Indeed it was! Somewhere between campaign briefs, I fell headfirst into learning Korean and Spanish, simultaneously, because apparently I don't do things halfway. What started as a curiosity quietly became a commitment. By the end of the year I was competing in Korean speaking and writing contests, won a speaking competition, and represented London at a learners' programme in South Korea. Spanish, I'll admit, is still catching up. But honestly? Learning to communicate across cultures has only made me a sharper marketer and I can't wait to bring that into the work ahead.

TUBI

Supported Tubi's UK brand awareness push through upper funnel paid social, collaborating on bold campaigns designed to capture attention at scale.
